pattern: Chunking the string into fixed-size windows in order leaves at most one short window at the very end, and padding just that one with the fill character is the whole trick.
hints: Work out how many groups of size k the string naturally breaks into, and notice that only the very last one can come up short.
Step through the string k characters at a time, slicing out one group per stride in order.
For that final slice, if it has fewer than k characters, top it up to length k with the fill character before adding it to the result -- every earlier group is already exactly k long.

edge_inputs.description: needs padding on the final short group
length divides evenly by k, no padding needed anywhere
k=1 turns every single character into its own group
k equals the whole string length, exactly one group and no padding
string shorter than k, the only group is mostly fill
the fill character also occurs naturally inside the string
the last group has only one real character before padding kicks in

stress.description: random lowercase strings with random group size and fill across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, 100)
    fill = rng.choice(letters)
    return [s, k, fill]
